16:9 top-down cinematic shot looking straight down into the broadcast studio from the reference image, from very high up. The man at his wooden desk is a small warm island of lamplight far below, microphone casting a long shadow. Keep his likeness. The impossibly tall city-night window is seen as a glowing blue wall running up one side of frame toward the camera, its panes stacking and compressing with the perspective. Palm canopies float between camera and floor like dark clouds. Vast empty floor swallowed in shadow, dust in the light column, slow-cinema loneliness, heavy grain.
